title: Van Allen Radiation Belts
subtitle: Earth's Invisible Shields
description: Explorer 1 and Explorer 3 revealed two toroidal zones of trapped charged particles around Earth — the Van Allen belts — reshaping how we understand near-Earth space.
impact: Defined the radiation environment spacecraft and astronauts must survive in orbit.

title: Solar Wind
subtitle: A Continuous Stream from the Sun
description: Luna 1 and later Mariner 2 confirmed that the Sun emits a steady flow of plasma — the solar wind — that fills the heliosphere and drives space weather.
impact: Explains auroras, comet tails, and how solar storms can disrupt technology on Earth.

title: Heliospheric Cosmic Rays
subtitle: Intensity Beyond the Bubble
description: Voyager 1 crossed the heliopause and measured a sharp rise in galactic cosmic-ray intensity — direct proof it had left the Sun's protective bubble.
impact: Mapped the edge of the heliosphere and how the Sun shields the solar system from cosmic radiation.

title: Lunar & Martian Water Ice
subtitle: Ice in the Inner Solar System
description: Missions from Chandrayaan-1 and LCROSS to Mars orbiters and polar landers confirmed water ice at the lunar poles and widespread ice on Mars — locked in soil, glaciers, and shadowed craters.
impact: Transformed plans for in-situ resources, habitability studies, and future human outposts.

title: Microgravity & Human Biology
subtitle: How Space Remakes the Body
description: Decades of ISS research show that long-duration microgravity drives bone loss, muscle atrophy, fluid shifts, immune changes, and vision issues — and that exercise and countermeasures can blunt many effects.
impact: Sets the medical baseline for deep-space crew health on trips to the Moon and Mars.
